title: Generators should explicitly yield a value
description: Generator functions should explicitly yield a value rather than yielding undefined. Empty yield statements (yield without a value) can lead to unexpected behavior and make the generator's purpose unclear. When a generator yields without a value, it produces undefined, which may not be the intended behavior and can cause issues in iteration logic.
recommendation: Always provide an explicit value when using yield statements. If you intend to yield undefined, use 'yield undefined' to make the intention clear.
impact: Code clarity and potential runtime issues

noncompliant: function* myGen() { for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { yield; } }
compliant: function* myGen() { for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) { yield i; } }

checks.message: Generator should explicitly yield a value instead of yielding undefined.
